checks.a_analysis: I conducted a thorough audit of all text elements in the JSON output, comparing them against the 38 pages of the source price list. The heading structure, category labels, product descriptions, and part numbers all have a clear basis in the original PDF. The document title in the metadata is acceptable as an AI-generated accessibility enhancement. I found no evidence of fabricated table entries, hallucinated paragraphs, or invented content.

The source document is an MPA Price List consisting entirely of part numbers, descriptions, and prices. It contains no phone numbers, email addresses, or physical addresses. I verified that the output does not hallucinate any contact information.

The JSON output contains 38 sections corresponding to the 38 pages of the source document (labeled Page 2 of 39 through Page 39 of 39). I verified that the content starts with the ArcGIS Desktop Concurrent Use section (source page 2) and concludes with the Advantage Program section (source page 39). All intermediate pages and their associated product tables are present.

I cross-referenced row counts for several pages. For example, Page 2 of 39 (JSON element 4) contains 32 data rows across three categories, and the JSON accurately reflects all 32. Page 39 of 39 (JSON element 38) contains 5 rows for the Advantage Program, all of which are present in the JSON. The tables maintain material parity with the source.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Each section in the JSON faithfully extracts the full set of part numbers, descriptions, and prices from the corresponding page. No significant blocks of descriptive text or entire price columns were omitted from the resulting tables.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: I observed minor OCR-related variations, such as the normalization of spacing in some product descriptions (e.g., 'Schematics for' vs 'Schematicsfor' in Image 1). These are minor reformatting differences that preserve the semantic meaning of the document and do not alter the critical information (prices and part numbers).

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON represents the sequence of tables found across the pages of the source document. There is no evidence of text blocks being duplicated incorrectly (e.g., repeated paragraphs or sections appearing twice when they exist only once in the source).

The document title elements are correctly identified as H1 and H2. Sub-headers within the tables are rendered as data rows, which is acceptable for this type of price list structure. No heading level skips (e.g., H1 to H3) were found in the structure.

The table structure is materialy corrupted. Throughout the document, the tables are defined with a 5-column header, but the rows in the table body fail to maintain this structure or use appropriate colspans. Category header rows (spanning the full width in the source) are rendered with a single cell having colspan: 1. Sub-header rows with two fields are rendered with two cells having colspan: 1 each. Standard data rows omit the empty leading column ('Section'), resulting in rows with only 4 cells instead of 5. This breaks the grid-alignment necessary for accessibility and data integrity.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF (e.g., Page 2) shows tables with 5 distinct columns (Section, Part Number, Description, Previous Price, 2025 Price). Category headers like 'ArcGIS Desktop and Extensions' span all columns.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the table at reading_order 4, row index 1 has only one cell with 'colspan': 1. Row index 2 has two cells with 'colspan': 1 each. Row index 3 has four cells with 'colspan': 1 each. Total spans do not sum to the 5 columns defined in the header row.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order follows the visual flow of the document from page to page and top to bottom within each page. The information flow is logical and matches the source's intended sequence.
The source document consists entirely of headings and tables. There are no standalone lists to evaluate.
No links are present in the source document, and no links were erroneously injected into the output JSON.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the price list is faithfully preserved in the HTML output. The 5-column table structure (Section, Part Number, Description, Previous Price, 2025 Price) matches the source PDF. Product categories and subcategories are clearly delineated with header rows spanning the table, maintaining the logical flow of the original document. The responsive layout appropriately adapts the fixed PDF pages into a continuous HTML view without loss of organizational intent.

The tables are rendered correctly. Column headers are clearly defined, and row data aligns perfectly under the respective columns. Cell alignment is consistent, making the complex price data easy to read. Long descriptions wrap correctly within their cells without overlapping or breaking the table structure. Prices correctly maintain their green color coding as seen in the source.

The source PDF pages consist entirely of text-based tables and headers. There are no meaningful images, charts, or diagrams in the source content that require conversion. No broken image placeholders are visible in the HTML output.

A thorough review of the HTML rendering confirms that no body content, such as price rows or description paragraphs, has been duplicated. While section headings appear in both the Table of Contents and the body of the document, this is an intentional accessibility feature and not a defect. The actual price list data flows sequentially without repetition.
